fraud_notes: ifsc_account_mismatch: IFSC on payslip (HDFC0001234) ≠ IFSC on bank statement (ICIC0009999). Statement belongs to a different account.
salary_cross_doc_mismatch: Payslip gross ₹1,44,000 vs bank salary credit ₹72,000 vs employment letter implied monthly ₹83,333. Payslips are inflated.
bank_date_range_fabricated: Statement header says '01-Oct-2025 to 31-Mar-2026' but all transactions are Jan–Mar 2026 only. Oct–Dec 2025 records were stripped.
bank_address_mismatch: IFSC 'HDFC0001234' belongs to HDFC Bank but the statement header says 'State Bank of India, Delhi Main Branch'. IFSC prefix and bank name are inconsistent.
bank_arithmetic_error: Row 5's running balance is inflated by ₹1,00,000. Prev balance + credit − debit ≠ stated balance. Row was manually edited.
salary_structure_low_basic: Basic is 10% of gross (should be 30–50%). HRA is 70% of gross (700% of basic, legal max = 50% of basic). PF obligation minimised; tax-free income maximised illegally.
hra_exceeds_basic: HRA ₹15,000 is 75% of basic ₹20,000. Legal max exemption under Sec 10(13A) for metro = 50% of basic. Excess HRA claimed to reduce taxable income.
extreme_backdating: Letter dated 15-Mar-2026; join date 01-Jan-2000 (26 years gap). Actual join per payslip: 2022-06-01. Employment backdated to meet bank's vintage-of-employment criterion.
pan_mismatch: Payslip PAN = EJEAN4646M; Form16 PAN = WBFCJ5615J. Identity fraud — Form 16 belongs to a different individual.
gift_letter_loan_disguise: Gift ₹1,800,000 = 90% of property ₹2,000,000. Same amount appears as large NEFT inflow in bank statement — possible undisclosed back-channel loan.
